body:
Hi Sam,

The sterling pendant necklace arrived this morning. The box looked fine from the outside but there was no padding inside, and the bail is bent where it meets the chain. The pendant itself looks okay otherwise.

I've got photos of the box and the bail if that helps. Let me know whether this is a repair or a replacement and how you'd like me to send it back.

Thanks,
Jenna

Jenna,

Sorry about that. The bail's an easy fix on my bench, no charge. Don't ship it loose like it came to you.

I'll email a prepaid FedEx label. Wrap the pendant in the pouch it came in, then bubble wrap, then the box. Turnaround is about a week once it's here. I'll also pad the return shipment better than that one was.

Send the photos when you can so I can file a claim with FedEx on my end.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Following up on the turquoise ring. FedEx tracking still hasn't updated in five days. It shows it left Albuquerque and then nothing. The estimated delivery date came and went on Tuesday.

I'm a little worried it's lost. Is there anything on your end that shows where it is? I can wait a few more days but wanted to flag it before too much time passes.

Thanks,
Patricia

Patricia,

I see the same stall on my end. It scanned out of the Albuquerque hub and hasn't moved, which usually means it's sitting somewhere it didn't get scanned, not lost.

I've opened a trace with FedEx. They give it 5 business days before they call it. If it doesn't move by then I'll make you a new ring and ship it priority at no cost - the original was insured.

I'll update you the moment the trace turns something up. Sorry for the wait.

Sam

Hi Sam,

The cuff bracelet I bought last month fits a little tight - I can get it on but it pinches. I'd like to send it in for a resize, maybe a quarter inch larger.

What's the process and how long does a resize take once you have it? And is there a charge? Happy to cover return shipping either way.

Thanks,
Gregory

Gregory,

A quarter inch is straightforward on a cuff - I just open the gap a touch and true it back up. First resize is free, you only cover shipping one way and I cover the return.

Turnaround is about a week on the bench once it's here. Wrap it well and use a small box, not a padded envelope - cuffs get crushed in mailers. I'll send a return label.

Let me know if you want it any specific final size and I'll measure to that.

Sam

Hi Sam,

That sounds good. A quarter inch larger should do it. I'll box it properly and use the label you send.

One question - it has a small turquoise stone set on top. Will the resize affect that at all, or is the stone clear of where you'd be working? Just want to make sure it comes back in one piece.

Thanks,
Gregory

Gregory,

The stone's set well clear of the opening, so the resize won't touch it. I work the back of the cuff, not the face.

I'll check the bezel while it's here anyway and tighten it if it needs it, no charge. Send it when you're ready - label's in your inbox.

Sam

Hi Sam,

The consignment shipment for Santa Fe Silver Gallery arrived. Most of it is perfect, but one of the inlay pendants has a cracked stone - looks like a hairline through the turquoise. I've set that one aside and haven't put it on the floor.

We have our opening this weekend, so I'd appreciate a quick turnaround on a replacement under warranty. Photos attached. Everything else looks great.

Thanks,
Diane

Diane,

Saw the photo - that's a transit crack, not a setting fault. Covered. I've got a matching pendant from the same stone lot ready to go.

It's going out FedEx priority today so you'll have it before the opening. Keep the cracked one aside and I'll include a return label in the box for it. No charge.

Sorry it arrived that way - I'm padding the gallery shipments heavier going forward.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Following up on my earring order before it ships - I need to change the delivery address. I've moved since I placed it.

New address:
412 Mesa Ridge Dr, Apt 6
Flagstaff, AZ 86001

Can you confirm it's updated before the package goes out? I'd hate for it to ship to the old place. Thanks for catching this in time.

Monica

Monica,

Updated. It's shipping to the Flagstaff address now, not the old one. It hadn't gone out yet so we're fine.

FedEx label will print with the new address and I'll send tracking once it's scanned. Should go out tomorrow.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Got the tracking, address shows correct. Thank you for turning that around fast - I was worried it had already shipped.

No rush on my end now that it's headed the right place. Appreciate it.

Monica

Hi Sam,

The FedEx box with the silver concho belt arrived crushed on one corner. The outer cardboard is caved in. I haven't opened it yet in case you need it photographed for a damage claim first.

Let me know how you want to handle it - whether to open it and check the belt, or hold it as-is. I can send photos of the box now.

Thanks,
Carl

Carl,

Send me the box photos first - I need them to file the FedEx claim. Get the shipping label and the crushed corner in frame.

Then go ahead and open it carefully. Concho belts are usually fine in there, the leather and silver take a knock better than the box does. If the conchos are bent or any stones are loose, photograph those too.

Either way you're covered. Let me know what's inside.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Opened it. Two of the conchos are dented and one stone is loose enough to rattle. The leather strap is okay. Photos of the box and the damaged conchos are attached.

Not the end of the world but it's not wearable as-is. Let me know whether you want it back for repair or you'd rather replace it.

Carl

Carl,

Send it back - I'd rather repair than remake that one, the leather's good and the silver work is sound under the dents. I can true the conchos and reset the loose stone. Comes back better than new.

The photos are enough for the FedEx claim. Return label's in your inbox. Use a sturdy box this time, not a mailer, and I'll cover shipping both ways. About a week on the bench once it lands.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Following up on my ring shipment. FedEx tracking says it was delivered Tuesday, but it never showed up at my door. I checked with the neighbors and the front office - nobody has it.

I think it was misdelivered or taken. Can you help me open a lost shipment claim? I really want this ring, it was for an anniversary. Let me know what you need from me.

Thanks,
Laura

Laura,

That's a "delivered but not received" - happens with misdelivery. I've opened the claim with FedEx; they'll check the GPS scan and the delivery photo if there is one.

It was insured, so here's what I'll do: I'll start making a replacement ring now rather than wait on FedEx, so you're not stuck while they investigate. If the original turns up you can keep both or send one back, your call.

Give me your size again to confirm and I'll get it on the bench today. Sorry this happened - anniversary or not, you should get the ring.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Following up on the sterling chain I bought from you. The clasp broke after about two weeks of normal wear - the spring ring just stopped catching and now it won't close at all. I wasn't rough with it.

I'd like to send it in for warranty repair. What do you need from me, and is there a cost? Happy to cover shipping out to you.

Thanks,
Evan

Evan,

A spring ring failing that fast is a defective clasp, not wear. Covered under warranty, no charge, and I'll cover shipping both ways.

I'll put on a heavier lobster clasp instead - it's sturdier than the spring ring and won't give you the same trouble. Return label's coming to your inbox. Small box, not an envelope.

Turnaround's a few days once it's here. I'll check the rest of the chain while it's on the bench.

Sam

Hi Sam,

That works, the lobster clasp sounds better anyway. I'll box it up and use the label you sent.

Thanks for making this easy - I was worried I'd be out a chain. Appreciate the warranty standing behind it.

Evan

Hi Sam,

Quick question on my pendant order - can you confirm the ship date and whether it's going FedEx ground or express? I'm trying to get it in hand before a birthday on the 5th.

If ground won't make it I'm happy to pay for express. Just let me know what's realistic.

Thanks,
Nathan

Nathan,

The pendant's finished and ready to box. Ground from Gallup to you is 3 business days, so if it ships tomorrow it lands before the 5th with a day to spare.

No need to pay for express - ground makes it. I'll send tracking once FedEx scans it. If it slips for any reason I'll upgrade it on my dime so you're not late for the birthday.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Perfect, ground it is. Thanks for checking the timing - that takes the pressure off.

Go ahead and ship whenever it's ready. I'll watch for the tracking.

Nathan

Nathan,

Shipped this morning, FedEx ground. Tracking's in your email. It's showing delivery on the 4th, so you're clear for the 5th.

It's packed well - pouch, bubble, box. Let me know it arrives in one piece.

Sam

Hi Sam,

The earrings arrived but one of the French ear wires is bent, so the pair doesn't hang even - one sits higher than the other. The stones and the silver look fine otherwise, it's just the wire.

Can these be repaired or exchanged? I've got a photo showing the bent wire next to the straight one. Let me know how you'd like to handle it.

Thanks,
Renee

Renee,

A bent ear wire is a quick fix - I'll just swap it for a fresh one so the pair hangs even. No charge, that's a transit bend not your doing.

Send them back in a small rigid box, not a padded mailer - mailers are what bend ear wires in the first place. Return label's in your inbox and I'll cover return shipping. A few days on the bench and they're back to you straight.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Different piece this time - the coral inlay bracelet. One of the coral stones is loose in its bezel and rattles a little when I move my wrist. Nothing's fallen out yet but I stopped wearing it so it doesn't.

I'd like it reset under warranty before I lose the stone. How should I ship it back to you?

Thanks,
Evan

Evan,

Good call stopping wear before it dropped. A loose bezel is covered - I'll reset the coral and burnish the bezel back down tight so it holds. No charge.

Ship it in a small box with the bracelet wrapped so the loose stone can't work itself out in transit. Return label's coming. I'll check the other stones in the inlay while it's here so you don't have a repeat.

About a week on the bench. I'll text when it ships back.

Sam

Hi Sam,

That sounds great, thank you. I'll box it up the way you said and keep the loose stone padded.

Good to know you'll check the rest of the inlay too. I'll watch for the return tracking. Appreciate you standing behind it.

Evan

Hi Sam,

Following up on my Etsy order for the silver and turquoise pendant. It's past the estimated delivery date and the FedEx tracking hasn't moved in several days - last scan just says it left the origin facility.

I'm starting to worry it's stuck or lost. Can you check on the shipment status? I'd appreciate any update before I keep waiting.

Thanks,
Claudia

Claudia,

I pulled it up - it's sitting at a sort facility without a new scan, which usually means it's delayed not lost. I've opened a trace with FedEx to push it along.

Give it a couple business days for the trace. If it doesn't move I'll remake the pendant and ship it priority at no cost - the order's insured either way. You won't be out the piece or the money.

I'll update you as soon as the trace turns something up. Sorry for the wait.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Thank you, that's reassuring. I'll give the trace a couple of days before worrying more. I really do want this pendant, so I appreciate you staying on it.

I'll keep an eye on the tracking and let you know if it starts moving on my end.

Claudia

Claudia,

It moved - scanned back into the network this morning and it's out for delivery to you tomorrow. The trace shook it loose.

Keep an eye out and let me know it arrives in good shape. If anything's off with the pendant when it lands, say the word and I'll make it right.

Sam
